title: microchilus elatus
intro: Microchilus elatus, also known as the "barkless orchid", is a type of flowering plant native to tropical Asia, specifically in the regions of India, Sri Lanka, and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits associated with Microchilus elatus include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. Microchilus elatus is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for consumption, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine in the form of poultices or topical creams.
